intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gentle, approachable visual language for health tracking over clinical sharp-edged design
facade.axisTargeted: Roundedness vs. sharpness in UI elements
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard components should use generous border-radius and curved shapes to feel supportive rather than sterile
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer crisp, minimal aesthetics with sharp corners or flat geometric precision for clarity and trust

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer gentle, approachable visual language for health tracking over clinical sharp-edged design
evidence.implication: Dashboard components should use generous border-radius and curved shapes to feel supportive rather than sterile

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gradual animated transitions between dashboard views over instant state changes
facade.axisTargeted: Motion continuity: animated transitions vs instant swaps
facade.label: Smooth fade transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Explore layered view transitions, progress animations, and micro-interactions that sustain user focus during data exploration
facade.rejectImplies: Prioritize snappy instant state changes, instant data loads, and minimal animation—users want efficiency and direct feedback over motion fluidity

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking long-term health experiments prefer seeing single key metrics in large, scannable format over detailed tables or written summaries
facade.label: Big numbers at a glance
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should emphasize large, prominent numbers (weight, days streak, test score) with minimal context; metric cards over text-heavy breakdowns
facade.rejectImplies: Users want context and nuance alongside numbers; need to show trend lines, confidence levels, or narrative explanation; single metrics feel oversimplified for health decisions

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking long-term health experiments prefer information grouped into discrete, generous visual blocks rather than dense rows or sidebar hierarchies
facade.axisTargeted: card-based vs list-based; density (spacious vs compact)
facade.label: Warm, spacious cards
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward card layouts with breathing room, possibly grid-based. Suggests user values visual scanning and self-contained experiment snapshots over tabular comparison.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer compact list views, sidebar organization, or dense data tables for quick reference and comparison across many experiments.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, reflective atmosphere for reviewing progress
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights about patterns
Whether user wants instant feedback and animations vs. static snapshots they control at their own pace

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, reflective atmosphere for reviewing progress
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights about patterns
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and animations vs. static snapshots they control at their own pace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A personal health dashboard benefits from gentle visual transitions that feel organic and human rather than stark or jarring, creating a calm, introspective mood suitable for long-term self-tracking.
facade.axisTargeted: Warm vs Cool / Organic vs Digital
facade.label: Soft gradients
facade.acceptImplies: Favor smooth color flows, blurred edges, nature-inspired palettes, and subtle animations that feel alive but unhurried.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers crisp geometry, high contrast, clinical clarity, or minimal transitions—prioritize information density and precision over atmospheric softness.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer warm, encouraging language and subtle celebration of small wins over clinical, neutral tracking language
facade.label: Cozy check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Voice should feel like a supportive friend noticing progress, not a medical system logging data
facade.rejectImplies: Voice should stay neutral and functional, letting the data speak for itself without emotional scaffolding

facade.hypothesis: Users find dark color schemes more suitable for health tracking dashboards because they reduce eye strain during frequent check-ins and feel less institutional than light interfaces
facade.axisTargeted: Color palette — dark vs. light dominant
facade.label: Dark backgrounds, light text
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should default to dark mode with high contrast text; night-time usage and sustained viewing are design priorities
facade.rejectImplies: Light or balanced color schemes work better; users associate dark interfaces with surveillance or feel they lack warmth for health contexts
